content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based curious agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new experiences an active learning agent encodes into its long-term memory, addressing the 2024-2025 SOTA challenge of catastrophic forgetting and memory bloat in scaling agent contexts. This framework grades unlabeled candidate experiences not by prediction error (the standard active learning heuristic) but by the agent's intrinsic curiosity signal, automatically retaining high-impact, surprising information that improves downstream task performance.
